---
title: Type Checking
---

MIKE IO has type annotations throughout the codebase and passes mypy in strict mode. This page covers the two places where type narrowing helps: `mikeio.open()` and `.geometry`.


## `mikeio.open()` returns a broad union

`mikeio.open()` is a factory function that returns different classes depending on the file extension. Since the filename is a runtime value, the type checker sees the full union:

```
Dfs0 | Dfs1 | Dfs2 | Dfs3 | Dfsu2DH | Dfsu2DV | Dfsu3D | DfsuSpectral | Mesh
```

If you know the file type, skip `open()` and construct the class directly:

```python
from mikeio import Dfs2

dfs = Dfs2("tide.dfs2")
# type checker sees: Dfs2 — full autocompletion
```

If you receive the object from other code, narrow with `isinstance`:

```python
import mikeio
from mikeio import Dfs2

dfs = mikeio.open("tide.dfs2")
assert isinstance(dfs, Dfs2)
```


## Narrowing geometry

`DataArray.geometry` and `Dataset.geometry` are typed as `GeometryType` — a union of all geometry classes. The type checker will catch typos like `.nxx`, but to get full autocompletion and precise error messages, narrow to the specific geometry:

```python
from mikeio import Dfs2, Grid2D

dfs = Dfs2("tide.dfs2")
ds = dfs.read()
assert isinstance(ds.geometry, Grid2D)
ds.geometry.nx  # autocompletion works, mypy suggests "nx" if you typo "nxx"
```


## File type → class → geometry mapping

| File extension | Dfs class | Geometry class |
|---|---|---|
| `.dfs0` | `Dfs0` | `Geometry0D` |
| `.dfs1` | `Dfs1` | `Grid1D` |
| `.dfs2` | `Dfs2` | `Grid2D` |
| `.dfs3` | `Dfs3` | `Grid3D` |
| `.dfsu` (2D horizontal) | `Dfsu2DH` | `GeometryFM2D` |
| `.dfsu` (3D) | `Dfsu3D` | `GeometryFM3D` |
| `.dfsu` (2D vertical) | `Dfsu2DV` | `GeometryFMVerticalProfile` |
| `.dfsu` (spectral) | `DfsuSpectral` | `GeometryFMPointSpectrum` / `GeometryFMLineSpectrum` / `GeometryFMAreaSpectrum` |
| `.mesh` | `Mesh` | `GeometryFM2D` |

All classes above can be imported directly from `mikeio`:

```python
from mikeio import Dfs2, Dfsu2DH, Grid2D, GeometryFM2D, Geometry0D
```

::: {.callout-tip}
If you use VS Code with the Pylance extension, `assert isinstance(...)` will immediately give you full autocompletion — no configuration needed.
:::
